title: CORE Enforcement Mapping Entry
description: Schema for each entry under `mappings:` in any .intent/enforcement/mappings/**/*.yaml file. An entry pairs a rule_id (the dict key) with the enforcement strategy that resolves it. `scope.applies_to` may be empty only for declared context-level dispatch (the check iterates globally, not per-file, e.g. contracts_gate.layer_scope_coherence, llm_gate.purity.logic_conservation) — the loader (rule_extractor.py) treats a missing or empty applies_to as 'match nothing' rather than the pre-#158 silent widen-to-all-of-src/ behavior, so an empty list is safe, not dangerous, and no longer needs the blanket minItems:1 this schema previously carried.

engine.description: The execution engine that resolves this rule. Examples: ast_gate, regex_gate, knowledge_gate, llm_gate, glob_gate, workflow_gate, passive_gate, advisory.

params.description: Engine-specific parameters. Shape is determined by the engine and is not constrained by this schema.

scope.description: Defines which files this rule examines. The key is required so that rule extraction never falls back to an implicit repo-wide default (issue #158); its value may be an empty list for declared context-level checks.

applies_to.description: Glob patterns (relative to repo root) for files this rule applies to. Empty is permitted only when the rule's engine/check_type is context-level (does not iterate per-file) — document why in a comment above the entry, per the two existing precedents (ADR-043 D7 / #310, and the layer_scope_coherence contracts_gate check).

excludes.description: Glob patterns to exclude from applies_to, for files/directories the rule does not conceptually apply to by definition (e.g. test files, __init__.py, a layer positively permitted to do what the rule forbids elsewhere). No governance debt implied — permanent, not tracked. For a file that DOES violate the rule and is temporarily permitted to keep violating it, use governed_exclusions instead (ADR-042 D3, ADR-049 D3, ADR-152).

governed_exclusions.description: Per-file exemption register (ADR-042 D3, generalized by ADR-152 to also carry ADR-049 D3's deadline-closure discipline). Each entry documents a file that currently violates this rule and why governance temporarily permits it. The audit pipeline (rule_extractor.py) honors each entry's `file` value as if it were listed under scope.excludes, suppressing the underlying violation for that path. Two closure shapes exist, discriminated by `closure_type`: 'condition' (ADR-042's original modularity shape — closes when an observable code condition changes) and 'deadline' (ADR-049 D3's shape — closes by a named date backed by a closure ADR). Required fields differ by shape; see the per-entry conditional requirements below.

file.description: Repo-relative path of the exempted source file.

closure_type.description: How this exemption is meant to close. 'condition': the exemption ends when an observable code condition changes (e.g. a class gains a second responsibility, or an audit path becomes operational) — requires class/category/removal_condition. 'deadline': the exemption ends on a named date backed by a closure ADR — requires deadline/closure_adr/tracking_issue (ADR-049 D3).

class.description: Name of the exempted top-level class within the file. Required when closure_type is 'condition'; not applicable to whole-file exemptions closed by deadline.

category.description: Why the class exceeds the LOC pre-selector despite single-responsibility status. facade: gateway whose size reflects surface breadth. algorithm: cohesive algorithm whose passes share state. catalog: collection of trivial parallel members (e.g. dataclass register or property table). Required when closure_type is 'condition'.

rationale.description: Why this exemption is temporarily accepted. Always required, regardless of closure_type.

removal_condition.description: The observable change that retires this exemption — typically the emergence of a second responsibility, or the llm_gate audit path becoming operational (ADR-042 D4). Required when closure_type is 'condition'.

deadline.description: ISO 8601 date by which this exemption must close (ADR-049 D3). A warning finding is emitted once passed with the closure ADR not yet accepted; the exemption stops suppressing the underlying violation 30 days after (the rule becomes blocking for this path exactly as if unexempted). Required when closure_type is 'deadline'.

closure_adr.description: The .specs/decisions/ ADR naming the file, the rule it bypasses, why the bypass is temporarily accepted, and the planned refactor that removes it (ADR-049 D3). Required when closure_type is 'deadline'.

tracking_issue.description: The GitHub issue tracking the closure work. Required when closure_type is 'deadline'.

requires_findings_from.description: Pre-selector dependency list introduced by ADR-043 D2. When non-empty, the rule's per-file scope is intersected with the set of files that have findings under the listed rule IDs in the current audit run. Empty intersection produces no work and no findings. Each entry must reference a rule ID declared elsewhere in `.intent/rules/`; cycles across these edges are rejected at rule extraction time. Not permitted on context-level engines (workflow_gate, knowledge_gate) — such rules do not iterate per-file and cannot be narrowed by a file set.
